title: Replace wave mode with 3D secondary mode
summary: Add a volumetric crystal field as the second 3D sculptural mode, ensuring all modes are point-based with 3D deformation and no flat/2D geometries remain. The crystal field system, shaders, and volumetric generators are already implemented; remaining work is test coverage for the crystal system, build verification, and runtime validation.

existing_implementation.crystalField_system: src/visual/systems/crystalField.ts — fully implemented with quantized noise, dual-axis twist, audio-reactive uniforms
existing_implementation.fragment_shader: src/visual/shaders/crystalWarp.frag.glsl — sharp edge falloff fragment shader
existing_implementation.volumetric_generator: src/visual/generators/volumetricPoints.ts — crystalCluster and geode shapes added
existing_implementation.mode_registration: src/main.ts lines 120-125 — crystal mode registered in createModeManager

steps.title: Verify no WaveField or 2D wave remnants exist
steps.description: Confirm there is no WaveField.ts, wave mode registration, or flat 2D line/plane geometry in any visual system. The codebase has been checked and no such files exist.

steps.title: Validate crystal field shader compilation
steps.description: Run the build to ensure crystalWarp.vert.glsl and crystalWarp.frag.glsl compile without errors. Verify no WebGL validation errors via the ShaderErrorCollector integration from US-048. All GLSL symbols (uniforms, attributes, varyings, locals) are declared and matched to BufferGeometry attributes in crystalField.ts.
checks: All 5 attributes (position, color, size, aHueOffset, aRandom) are set on BufferGeometry before render
All 17 uniforms are initialized in the uniforms object
noise3d.glsl is prepended to vertex shader for fbm3/snoise/curl3 availability

steps.title: Write crystal field unit tests
steps.description: Create tests/visual/systems/crystalField.test.ts covering: init creates Points mesh with correct geometry attributes, draw updates all uniforms from FrameState, setOpacity updates uOpacity uniform, cleanup removes mesh and disposes resources, point count respects density and structureComplexity, shape selection is restricted to crystalCluster/geode.
test_ids: T-044-10: init adds THREE.Points to scene
T-044-11: geometry has all 5 required attributes (position, color, size, aHueOffset, aRandom)
T-044-12: point count scales with density × structureComplexity
T-044-13: draw updates uBassEnergy and uTrebleEnergy from frame params
T-044-14: draw updates uPointerPos from frame pointer coordinates
T-044-15: draw updates evolved params (noiseFrequency, radialScale, twistStrength, fieldSpread)
T-044-16: setOpacity sets uOpacity uniform value
T-044-17: cleanup removes mesh from scene and disposes geometry and material
T-044-18: cleanup nullifies all internal references
T-044-19: config.maxPoints caps effective point count

steps.title: Write crystal mode integration tests
steps.description: Add integration tests verifying crystal mode works within mode manager: mode switching transitions preserve audio pipeline, crystal mode receives correct FrameState during crossfade, opacity ramps during transition.
test_ids: T-044-30: crystal mode initializes correctly via mode manager
T-044-31: mode transition to crystal preserves bassEnergy/trebleEnergy flow
T-044-32: crystal setOpacity is called during crossfade transition
T-044-33: crystal cleanup is called when mode manager cycles away

steps.title: Verify all four modes are volumetric and 3D
steps.description: Audit all registered modes (particles, ribbon, pointcloud, crystal) to confirm none use 2D line geometries or flat plane geometries. All must use THREE.Points or THREE.Mesh with 3D vertex displacement. This is an acceptance-criteria gate.
checks: particleField uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with 3D displacement shader
ribbonField uses THREE.Mesh + BufferGeometry with 3D helical vertex shader
pointCloud uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with FBM displacement shader
crystalField uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with quantized displacement shader
No THREE.Line, THREE.LineSegments, or PlaneGeometry used in any mode

steps.title: Run full build and test suite
steps.description: Execute npm run build and npm run test to verify: production build succeeds, all tests pass (including new crystal tests), no console errors, no shader compile errors.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Second mode is volumetric and point-base: Crystal field uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with crystalCluster/geode volumetric shapes (step 2, 5)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Mode uses 3D deformation: crystalWarp.vert.glsl implements quantized FBM displacement, dual-axis twist, bass expansion, treble jitter — all in 3D (step 2)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.No 2D line or flat plane geometries rema: All four modes verified as 3D volumetric (step 5). No WaveField.ts exists (step 1)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Mode switching preserves audio pipeline: Mode manager crossfade passes bassEnergy/trebleEnergy through FrameState during transitions (step 4)

risks.risk: Shader compile error on specific GPU/driver
risks.mitigation: ShaderErrorCollector from US-048 catches compile errors; initAllForValidation pre-compiles all mode shaders on startup

risks.risk: Performance regression with 4 modes instead of 3
risks.mitigation: Crystal uses 0.8× point count of pointCloud; only one mode renders at a time (plus brief overlap during crossfade)
